context: Isolated fused TQ3 attention wins at long seq_k; E2E vLLM tok/s stays flat when hipBLASLt/GEMM dominates rocprof top-kernels (see results/story2_rocprof_bucket_compare.json after running story2_rocprof_summarize.py).
microbench: kernels/ffn_fused_swiglu_spike.py — Triton SwiGLU elementwise fusion vs separate SiLU*mul; not wired into vLLM.
go_criteria: rocprof bucket_compare shows activation_elementwise + related non-GEMM share >= 5% top-kernel time on decode path for target model shape, OR
torch.profiler / Nsight shows SwiGLU-bound segments on MLP hot path for the same build as serving benchmarks
no_go_criteria: gemm_hipblaslt + gemm_general account for >= 85% of top-kernel cumulative time across fp16 and turboquant_fused at kv-heavy shapes with no material activation bucket
ffn_fused_swiglu_spike shows <5% speedup at decode-shaped (M, N) vs eager PyTorch for representative dtypes
integration_locus: Prefer upstream vLLM custom op / plugin or ROCm-optimized MLP path; full MLP rewrite in amd-experiments is out of scope without fork alignment.
next_action_if_go: Prototype fused SwiGLU + quant matmul boundary in a vLLM fork or extension API; re-run bench_vllm_turboquant_ab + bench_vllm_rocprof_timeline --kv-heavy-story2.
